original text:
Las Vegas, Nevada - September 14, 2004 - YesSoftware today announced DemoCharge 2004, an essential product for creating animated demos for use in Web content, emails, documents, tutorials, and training and help materials. It records the use of any application or onscreen activity, including all visible mouse movements, and instantly creates demos in DemoGIF (animated GIFs) format. File sizes are small enough to email, publish on the Web or include in documentation. DemoCharge Professional version will also support Macromedia® Flash® format and Java Applets and is scheduled for release Q1 of 2005.
Demos can be created in three easy steps that even the most novice user can complete. First, record the use of any application or onscreen activity. Second, enhance the individual frames in the intuitive workspace with special effects such as customized text captions, buttons, images, and highlights to attract attention to important points. Third, have DemoCharge generate an animated DemoGIF.
Quickly create office demos to answer those "how do I" questions about Word, Excel, and other common applications. Email the compact DemoCharge animation to the entire office.
Convey the value of your company's products, services, or Web site through DemoCharge's powerful animated demonstrations.
Easily email or publish online or offline DemoCharge animations to answer FAQ's, train new customers, and avoid costly phone support.
"Cluttered Web pages or wordy emails, tutorials, and Help files with too much written information tend to confuse people," said Konrad Musial, CEO of YesSoftware. "With DemoCharge, this information is conveyed visually to reduce confusion, give more detailed explanation, enable quick decision-making, and transcend language barriers. It's easy-to-use and has endless applications that are helpful to the casual computer user, technical support, sales and marketing professionals, and skilled developers."
YesSoftware offers two editions of DemoCharge 2004 for PC computers running Microsoft® Windows®. DemoCharge 2004 Standard Edition is available for $ 99.95 per license. DemoCharge 2004 Co-branded Edition, best for personal, academic and nonprofit use, if available for $ 49.95 per license and places the message, "Created with DemoCharge" at the bottom of each generated demo. DemoCharge Professional Edition will be available for purchase Q1 2005 for $ 199.95 per license.
Multi-seat licenses are also available. For a complete list of features, benefits, and pricing or to download a free 20-day trial version of DemoCharge please visit the YesSoftware Web site at http://www.yessoftware.com/products/.
All YesSoftware products are available for purchase through the YesSoftware Web site at www.yessoftware.com in addition to being sold through YesSoftware's international partners. For more information, please visit http://www.yessoftware.com/ or call 888-241-7338 from within the United States, or +1-702-798-4370 for international calls.
YesSoftware is an award-winning provider of various software solutions that puts the power of the Internet into the hands of the people by taking difficult and monotonous tasks and turning them into enjoyable, visual experiences.
Business users, developers, Webmaster, consultants, students, and teachers around the world use YesSoftware products to create Internet experiences.
YesSoftware, CodeCharge, CodeCharge Studio and DemoCharge are trademarks of YesSoftware, Inc. All other trademarks are the property of their respective owners.

original text:
Going to look at a 2005 vectra cdti Sri 150 at the weekend, and just wondering if there are any common problems I should be looking for (and how to spot them!). I seem to remember something about 'swirl pots', but to be honest don't know what they are or what I'd be looking for. Any advice greatfully received. Many thanks.
Best advice...Dont buy one! stick to a petrol, it will cost you less in the long run.
Maybe a bit of good advice there! I do love the way diesels drive especially the v6 but am dreading anything going wrong with her as they say they are expensive to fix. Diesel used to be cheap once over not now!
I own one ..... I wouldn't get another older one, half of it newer than when I bought it!
Maybe one of the later ones if the price was right .... But I'd still have a reserve pot for things like the DMF etc.
Shame because when it is running I love it.
Ahh, not quite the advice I was expecting. Well, that's me thoroughly put off! My other thought was to spend more and get an Insignia SRI, although again I'd be looking at diesel. Hopefully the newer design would be more reliable?!
Next we have the EGR valve which is a £100 replacement or a freebie clean up grease and replace.
Final one is again very common to every new gen diesel that is the dual mass flywheel clutch and slave. This is a biggie and can run about £1000 but many have had theirs done for about £600 all in ans some do it themselves for about £400.
Luck of the draw with them ..... Have a look around some have run like a dream for thousands of miles, however the common faults are expensive, if the car you have been looking at has had the work ..... Maybe worth it?
Def check on inlet manifold, clutch dmf and give it a good run looking for judders from engine and knocks from bushes and springs ...... What I can think of to look more in depth at on the cdti apart from the norms.
my Cdti V6 runs like a dream.Its a very responsive car and get 40+ to the gallon,not bad for a 3.0 V6. As with any modern car if they fail they can cost money its not just Vectras.Make sure you get one that has a good history, lowest miles and owners possible.
Go for it mate..the 1.9cdti is as reliable as any other car out there..the forums are here for peoople with interest in the car's and also alot of people asking for advise with problems..look at the bmw/audi/merc's/fords/ etc etc forums,there full of problems also,thats what there here for.And also look at why company's buy them for fleet vehicles,there mile munchers with low cost problems,why else would they buy them if they cost them a fortune to maintain..There is always a risk that you buy a lemon as with any car..good luck,but i recommend the 1.9cdti 07 plate onwards as i have one with no probs so far..TOUCH WOOD lol.
Vectra 1.9 CDTi buying advice..
Looking at buying a 3.0V6 CDTi:Advice please!!!
